mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over 3 months signals a surge in housing demand that drives up aggregate consumption and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > HOUST_mean + 2*HOUST_stddev over 3 months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) over 2 months signals a contraction in supply capacity that reduces the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < INDPRO_mean - 1*INDPRO_stddev over 2 months are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5-percentage-point rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over 1 month signals a tightening labor market that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > UNRATE_mean + 0.5*UNRATE_stddev over 1 month are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in the real GDP (GDPC1) over 4 months signals a surge in aggregate demand that drives up S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > GDPC1_mean + 1*GDPC1_stddev over 4 months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5-percentage-point rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over 1 month signals a tightening monetary cycle that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > FEDFUNDS_mean + 0.5*FEDFUNDS_stddev over 1 month are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) and a 1-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over 2 months signals a contraction in supply capacity and a surge in housing demand that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < INDPRO_mean - 1*INDPRO_stddev and HOUST > HOUST_mean + 1*HOUST_stddev over 2 months are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in the real personal income (RSAFS) over 1 month signals a surge in aggregate demand that drives up S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > RSAFS_mean + 1*RSAFS_stddev over 1 month are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
